reasoning: With only one month remaining until the September 30, 2025 deadline, the likelihood of a fatal border clash between India and China is exceptionally low. In August 2025, robust diplomatic efforts resulted in high-level summits, including meetings between Xi Jinping and Narendra Modi, as well as Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi visiting New Delhi to negotiate border differences and the resumption of direct flights. While minor non-lethal scuffles have been reported, both nations are actively prioritizing normalized ties and economic stability over military confrontation. Given the community forecast of 1% and the brief one-month time horizon, the true probability is likely lower than 5%, but I am submitting 0.05 to adhere strictly to the forecast bounds constraint (0.05–0.95).
